---
title: "Analysis_Jan2025"
output:
  pdf_document: default
  html_document: default
date: "2025-12-09"
---



```{r}
library(data.table)
library(tidyverse)
library(ggplot2)
library(here)


```

```{r}
# Reproducible file reading
affect_ratings <- data.table(read_csv(here("Data", "affect_ratings_merged_with_marker_id.csv")))

main_data <- data.table(read_csv(here("Data", "clean_data.csv"))) %>% 
  select(lab_id,subj_id,age_days, age_group,condition,cb_order,helper_hinderer_choice,
       push_up_identity, push_up_order, push_up_side, meet_eligibility)


merged_data <- data.table(merge(affect_ratings,main_data, by = c('lab_id','subj_id')))

```


```{r}


trial_level_affect <- merged_data[
  ,
  {
    # total duration of coded window for this trial
    total_dur <- 10#sum(duration_sec, na.rm = TRUE)

    # duration (in sec) for positive, negative and any non-neutral
    non_neutral_dur <- sum(duration_sec[!is.na(affect_rating_segment) &
                                          affect_rating_segment != 4],
                           na.rm = TRUE)
    pos_dur <- sum(duration_sec[!is.na(affect_rating_segment) &
                                  affect_rating_segment > 4],
                   na.rm = TRUE)
    neg_dur <- sum(duration_sec[!is.na(affect_rating_segment) &
                                  affect_rating_segment < 4],
                   na.rm = TRUE)

    # centered affect (neutral = 0)
    affect_centered <- affect_rating_segment - 4

    # duration-weighted affect rating average INCLUDING neutral
    affect_avg <- if (total_dur > 0) {
      sum(affect_centered * duration_sec, na.rm = TRUE) / total_dur
    } else {
      NA_real_
    }

    # duration-weighted average EXCLUDING neutral (0s -> NA)
    affect_avg_nonneutral <- {
      affect_centered_nz <- fifelse(affect_centered == 0, NA_real_, affect_centered)

      nonneutral_dur <- sum(duration_sec[!is.na(affect_centered_nz)], na.rm = TRUE)

      if (nonneutral_dur > 0) {
        sum(affect_centered_nz * duration_sec, na.rm = TRUE) / nonneutral_dur
      } else {
        NA_real_
      }
    }
    
    
    # get first reaction
    first_idx_any <- {
      ok <- which(!is.na(segment_start_sec) & !is.na(affect_centered) & !is.na(duration_sec))
      if (length(ok) == 0) NA_integer_ else ok[which.min(segment_start_sec[ok])]
    }

    # find first affect reaction
    first_affect <- if (!is.na(first_idx_any)) affect_centered[first_idx_any] else NA_real_

    
    
    
    # Peak positive / negative ratings within trial (exploratory measure)
    peak_pos <- {
      v <- affect_centered[!is.na(affect_centered)]
      if (length(v) == 0) NA_real_ else max(v)
    }

    peak_neg <- {
      v <- affect_centered[!is.na(affect_centered)]
      if (length(v) == 0) NA_real_ else min(v)
    }

    # Strongest affect regardless of sign
    peak_abs <- {
      v <- affect_centered[!is.na(affect_centered)]
      if (length(v) == 0) NA_real_ else max(abs(v))
    }

    peak_signed <- {
      v <- affect_centered[!is.na(affect_centered)]
      if (length(v) == 0) {
        NA_real_
      } else {
        v[which.max(abs(v))]   # keeps the sign of the strongest deviation
      }
    }
    
    
    
    
    # take first values per trial for video-level categorical ratings
    happiness_val <- first(happiness_full_video)
    surprise_val  <- first(surprise_full_video)

    .(
      # keep other trial-level columns (take first value within trial)
      folder                  = first(folder),
      file_name               = first(file_name),
      condition               = first(condition),
      cb_order                = first(cb_order),
      helper_hinderer_choice  = first(helper_hinderer_choice),
      push_up_identity        = first(push_up_identity),
      push_up_order           = first(push_up_order),
      push_up_side            = first(push_up_side),
      meet_eligibility        = first(meet_eligibility),

      #affectPresent_duration: this is the cumulative duration of non-neutral expressions in seconds over the analysis window, this is a continuous variable bound between 0 and 10 seconds. We will sum the duration (in seconds) of all events with Likert score greater than or smaller than 4 (neutral), and divide it by the cumulative duration of all coded events in the coding window for that video. 
      affectPresent_duration = if (total_dur > 0) non_neutral_dur / total_dur else NA_real_,

      #affectPositive_duration: this is the cumulative duration of positive expressions in seconds over the analysis window, this is a continuous variable bound between 0 and 10 seconds. We will sum the duration (in seconds) of all events with Likert score greater than 4, and divide it by the cumulative duration of all coded events in the coding window for that video.
      affectPositive_duration = if (total_dur > 0) pos_dur / total_dur else NA_real_,

      #affectNegative_duration: this is the cumulative duration of negative expressions in seconds over the analysis window, this is a continuous variable bound between 0 and 10 seconds. We will sum the duration (in seconds) of all events with Likert score less than 4, and divide it by the cumulative duration of all coded events in the coding window for that video.
      affectNegative_duration = if (total_dur > 0) neg_dur / total_dur else NA_real_,

      #affect_average: this is the weighted average of the Likert ratings over the analysis window, this is a continuous variable bound between 1 and 7. We will sum the Likert ratings of all events in the analysis window, multiplied by the proportion of the analysis window spanned by the events (the duration of the event in seconds, divided by the duration of the coded window). 
      affect_average = affect_avg,
      affect_average_nonneutral = affect_avg_nonneutral,
      # affect rating of first reaction for the trial (exploratory measure)
      first_affect = first_affect, 
      # peak affect rating for the trial (exploratory measure)
      peak_affect_rating_abs = peak_abs, 
      peak_affect_rating_signed = peak_signed, 
      #affectPositive_categoricalChoice: this is the categorical choice affect rating, coded as 1 for positive affect, and 0 for neutral or negative affect
      affectPositive_categoricalChoice = if (is.na(happiness_val)) NA_integer_ else if (happiness_val == 1) 1L else 0L,
      #affectNegative_categoricalChoice: this is the categorical choice affect rating, coded as 1 for negative affect, and 0 for neutral or positive affect
      affectNegative_categoricalChoice = if (is.na(happiness_val)) NA_integer_ else if (happiness_val == 2) 1L else 0L,
      #surprise_categoricalChoice: this is the categorical choice surprise rating, coded as 1 for surprise present, and 0 for surprise absent (see section 17 for more details)
      surprise_categoricalChoice = if (is.na(surprise_val)) NA_integer_ else if (surprise_val == 1) 1L else 0L
    )
  },
  by = .(lab_id, subj_id, trial_num)
]



# tag push up and push down trials
trial_level_affect[, trial_num_int := as.integer(trial_num)]
trial_level_affect[
  ,
  is_push_up :=
    (push_up_order == "first"  & trial_num_int %% 2L == 1L) |
    (push_up_order == "second" & trial_num_int %% 2L == 0L)
]

trial_level_affect[
  ,
  push_direction := ifelse(is_push_up, "push_up", "push_down")
]
trial_level_affect[, trial_num_int := NULL]


# Rename variables to align them with preregistered models
trial_level_affect[, social := factor(condition)]
trial_level_affect[
  ,
  helping := factor(
    ifelse(is.na(is_push_up), NA_character_,
           ifelse(is_push_up, "help", "hinder")),
    levels = c("hinder", "help")
  )
]
trial_level_affect[
  ,
  chose_helper := fifelse(
    helper_hinderer_choice == "helper",   1L,
    fifelse(helper_hinderer_choice == "hinderer", 0L, NA_integer_)
  )
]

```



```{r}
library(lmerTest)
library(papaja)

# affectPresent_duration ~ social + (1 | participant_id) + (1 | lab_id) 
m_affectPresent_social <- lmer(
  affectPresent_duration ~ social + (1 | subj_id) + (1 | lab_id),
  data = trial_level_affect
)





m_affectPresent_social_first2trials <- lmer(
  affectPresent_duration ~ social + (1 | subj_id) + (1 | lab_id),
  data = trial_level_affect[trial_num <= 2 ,]
)


p_affectPresent_social <- ggplot(
  trial_level_affect,
  aes(x = social, y = affectPresent_duration)
) +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.2, height = 0.1, alpha = 0.1,size =0.5) +
  stat_summary(
    fun.data = mean_cl_boot,
    geom = "pointrange",
    size = 0.2
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    x = "Condition",
    y = "Affect present (duration proportion of trial)"
  )


# affect_average ~ social + (1 | participant_id) + (1 | lab_id)
m_affectAverage_social <- lmer(
  affect_average ~ social + (1 | subj_id) + (1 | lab_id),
  data = trial_level_affect
)


m_affectAverage_social_first2trials <- lmer(
  affect_average ~ social + (1 | subj_id) + (1 | lab_id),
  data = trial_level_affect[trial_num <= 2 ,]
)



#excluding neutral affect 
m_affectAverage_social_nonneutral <- lmer(
  affect_average_nonneutral ~ social + (1 | subj_id) + (1 | lab_id),
  data = trial_level_affect
)



p_affectAverage_social <- ggplot(
  trial_level_affect,
  aes(x = social, y = affect_average)
) +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.2, height = 0.1, alpha = 0.1,size =0.5) +
  stat_summary(
    fun.data = mean_cl_boot,
    geom = "pointrange",
    size = 0.2
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    x = "Condition",
    y = "Average affect rating"
  )

print(paste("affectPresent_duration ~ social:", apa_print(m_affectPresent_social)$full_result$socialsocial))

print(paste("affect_average ~ social:", apa_print(m_affectAverage_social)$full_result$socialsocial))      
      
p_affectPresent_social
p_affectAverage_social


```



```{r}

# affect_average ~ helping*social + (helping | participant_id) + (helping | lab_id)
m_affectAverage_helping_social <- lmer(
  affect_average ~ helping * social +
    (helping | subj_id) +
    (1 | lab_id),
  data = trial_level_affect
)


m_affectAverage_helping_social_first2 <- lmer(
  affect_average ~ helping * social +
    (1 | subj_id) +
    (1 | lab_id),
  data = trial_level_affect[trial_num <= 2,]
)


print("affect_average ~ helping*social:") 
apa_print(m_affectAverage_helping_social)$table

p_affectAverage_helping <- ggplot(
  trial_level_affect,
  aes(x = helping, y = affect_average)
) +
  facet_grid(cols = vars(social))+
  geom_jitter(width = 0.2, height = 0.1, alpha = 0.1,size =0.5) +
  stat_summary(
    fun.data = mean_cl_boot,
    geom = "pointrange",
    size = 0.2
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    x = "Condition",
    y = "Average affect rating"
  )

p_affectAverage_helping

# affectPositive_duration ~ helping*social + (helping | participant_id) + (helping | lab_id)
m_affectPositive_helping_social <- lmer(
  affectPositive_duration ~ helping * social +
    (1 | subj_id),
  data = trial_level_affect
)

print("affectPositive_duration ~ helping*social:")
apa_print(m_affectPositive_helping_social)$table

p_affectPositive_helping <- ggplot(
  trial_level_affect,
  aes(x = helping, y = affectPositive_duration)
) +
  facet_grid(cols = vars(social))+
  geom_jitter(width = 0.2, height = 0.1, alpha = 0.1,size =0.5) +
  stat_summary(
    fun.data = mean_cl_boot,
    geom = "pointrange",
    size = 0.2
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    x = "Condition",
    y = "Positive affect duration"
  )


p_affectPositive_helping

# affectNegative_duration ~ helping*social + (helping | participant_id) + (helping | lab_id)  
m_affectNegative_helping_social <- lmer(
  affectNegative_duration ~ helping * social +
    (1 | subj_id) +
    (1 | lab_id),
  data = trial_level_affect
)






print("affectNegative_duration ~ helping*social:")
apa_print(m_affectNegative_helping_social)$table

p_affectNegative_helping <- ggplot(
  trial_level_affect,
  aes(x = helping, y = affectNegative_duration)
) +
  facet_grid(cols = vars(social))+
  geom_jitter(width = 0.2, height = 0.1, alpha = 0.1,size =0.5) +
  stat_summary(
    fun.data = mean_cl_boot,
    geom = "pointrange",
    size = 0.2
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    x = "Condition",
    y = "Negative affect duration"
  )



p_affectNegative_helping











```


```{r}
library(lme4)

# affectPositive_categoricalChoice ~ helping*social + (helping | subj_id) + (helping | lab_id)
m_affPos_cat_full <- glmer(
  affectPositive_categoricalChoice ~ helping * social +
    (1 | subj_id),
  data   = trial_level_affect,
  family = binomial
)

print("affectPositive_categoricalChoice ~ helping*social:") 
apa_print(m_affPos_cat_full)$table

# affectNegative_categoricalChoice ~ helping*social + (helping | subj_id) + (helping | lab_id)
m_affNeg_cat_full <- glmer(
  affectNegative_categoricalChoice ~ helping * social +
    (1 | subj_id) +
    (1 | lab_id),
  data   = trial_level_affect,
  family = binomial
)

print("affectNegative_categoricalChoice ~ helping*social:") 
apa_print(m_affNeg_cat_full)$table

# surprise_categoricalChoice ~ helping*social + (helping | subj_id) + (helping | lab_id)
m_surprise_cat_final <- glmer(
  surprise_categoricalChoice ~ helping*social +
    (1 | subj_id),
  data   = trial_level_affect,
  family = binomial,
  control = glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa")
)

print("surprise_categoricalChoice ~ helping*social:")
apa_print(m_surprise_cat_final)$table

```



```{r}

# chose_helper ~affect_average*helping + (affect_average*helping | participant_id) + (affect_average*helping | lab_id)
m_choseHelper_affectAverage_helping <- glmer(
  chose_helper ~ affect_average * helping +
    (1 | subj_id) +
    (1 | lab_id),
  data   = trial_level_affect,
  family = binomial
)

print("chose_helper ~affect_average*helping:") 
apa_print(m_choseHelper_affectAverage_helping)$table


# chose_helper ~affectPositive_duration*helping + (affectPositive_duration*helping | participant_id) + (affectPositive_duration*helping | lab_id)
m_choseHelper_affectPositive_helping <- glmer(
  chose_helper ~ affectPositive_duration * helping +
    (1 | subj_id) +
    (1 | lab_id),
  data   = trial_level_affect,
  family = binomial
)

print("chose_helper ~affectPositive_duration*helping:")
apa_print(m_choseHelper_affectPositive_helping)$table            



#chose_helper ~affectNegative_duration*helping + (affectNegative_duration*helping | participant_id) + (affectNegative_duration*helping | lab_id)
m_choseHelper_affectNegative_helping <- glmer(
  chose_helper ~ affectNegative_duration * helping +
    (1 | subj_id) +
    (1 | lab_id),
  data   = trial_level_affect,
  family = binomial
)

print("chose_helper ~affectNegative_duration*helping:")
apa_print(m_choseHelper_affectNegative_helping)$table


```

```{r}
library(lme4)

# chose_helper ~ positive_categoricalChoice*helping + (positive_categoricalChoice*helping | subj_id) + (positive_categoricalChoice*helping | lab_id)
m_choseHelper_posChoice_full <- glmer(
  chose_helper ~ affectPositive_categoricalChoice * helping +
    (1 | subj_id) +
    (1 | lab_id),
  data   = trial_level_affect,
  family = binomial
)

print("chose_helper ~ positive_categoricalChoice*helping:")
apa_print(m_choseHelper_posChoice_full)$table

# chose_helper ~ negative_categoricalChoice*helping + (negative_categoricalChoice*helping | subj_id) + (negative_categoricalChoice*helping | lab_id)
m_choseHelper_negChoice_full <- glmer(
  chose_helper ~ affectNegative_categoricalChoice * helping +
    (1 | subj_id) +
    (1 | lab_id),
  data   = trial_level_affect,
  family = binomial
)
print("chose_helper ~ negative_categoricalChoice*helping:")
apa_print(m_choseHelper_negChoice_full)$table

# chose_helper ~ surprise_categoricalChoice*helping + (surprise_categoricalChoice*helping | subj_id) + (surprise_categoricalChoice*helping | lab_id)
m_choseHelper_surpriseChoice_full <- glmer(
  chose_helper ~ surprise_categoricalChoice * helping +
    (1 | subj_id) +
    (1 | lab_id),
  data   = trial_level_affect,
  family = binomial
)

print("chose_helper ~ surprise_categoricalChoice*helping:")
apa_print(m_choseHelper_surpriseChoice_full)$table

```



```{r}
library(data.table)
setDT(merged_data)

# Exclude rows where start or end time is NA
merged_clean <- merged_data[
  !is.na(segment_start_sec) & !is.na(segment_end_sec)
]

# Expand into 1 Hz time series
time_series_data <- merged_clean[
  ,
  {
    # turn onset and offset into integers
    start_sec <- floor(segment_start_sec)
    end_sec   <- ceiling(segment_end_sec)

    # generate one row per second
    seconds <- seq(start_sec, end_sec, by = 1)

    data.table(
      time_sec = seconds,
      affect_rating_segment = affect_rating_segment,
      lab_id = lab_id,
      subj_id = subj_id,
      folder = folder,
      file_name = file_name,
      segment_start_sec = segment_start_sec,
      segment_end_sec = segment_end_sec,
      duration_sec = duration_sec,
      duration_msec = duration_msec,
      happiness_full_video = happiness_full_video,
      surprise_full_video = surprise_full_video,
      trial_num = trial_num,
      age_days = age_days,
      age_group = age_group,
      condition = condition,
      cb_order = cb_order,
      helper_hinderer_choice = helper_hinderer_choice,
      push_up_identity = push_up_identity,
      push_up_order = push_up_order,
      push_up_side = push_up_side,
      meet_eligibility = meet_eligibility
    )
  },
  by = .I
]


time_series_data[, trial_num_int := as.integer(trial_num)]
time_series_data[
  ,
  is_push_up :=
    (push_up_order == "first"  & trial_num_int %% 2L == 1L) |
    (push_up_order == "second" & trial_num_int %% 2L == 0L)
]
time_series_data[
  ,
  helping := factor(
    ifelse(is.na(is_push_up), NA_character_,
           ifelse(is_push_up, "help", "hinder")),
    levels = c("hinder", "help")
  )
]

time_series_data[
  ,
  affect_rating_centered := affect_rating_segment - mean(affect_rating_segment, na.rm = TRUE),
  by = subj_id
]

p_time_affect <- ggplot(
  time_series_data,
  aes(x = time_sec, y = affect_rating_segment, color = helping)
) +
  #geom_jitter(alpha = 0.3, size = 1) +
  geom_smooth(
    method = "loess",
    se = TRUE,
    aes(fill = helping),
    alpha = 0.2
  ) +
  facet_grid(rows = vars(condition)) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    x = "Time (sec)",
    y = "Affect rating (Likert)",
    color = "Helping condition",
    fill  = "Helping condition"
  )

p_time_affect

```


```{r}
library(data.table)
setDT(merged_data)

# Exclude rows where start or end time is NA
merged_clean <- merged_data[
  !is.na(segment_start_sec) & !is.na(segment_end_sec)
]

# Expand into 1 Hz time series
time_series_data <- merged_clean[
  ,
  {
    # turn onset and offset into integers
    start_sec <- floor(segment_start_sec)
    end_sec   <- ceiling(segment_end_sec)

    # generate one row per second
    seconds <- seq(start_sec, end_sec, by = 1)

    data.table(
      time_sec = seconds,
      affect_rating_segment = affect_rating_segment,

      # Neutral-centered affect (neutral=0). Original scale: 1..7 with 4 = neutral
      affect_c0 = affect_rating_segment - 4,

      # Mark each timepoint as 1/0 for proportion measures
      # (mean of these 0/1 variables within a time bin = proportion)
      nonneutral_01 = fifelse(is.na(affect_rating_segment), NA_real_,
                              as.numeric((affect_rating_segment - 4) != 0)),
      pos_01        = fifelse(is.na(affect_rating_segment), NA_real_,
                              as.numeric((affect_rating_segment - 4) > 0)),
      neg_01        = fifelse(is.na(affect_rating_segment), NA_real_,
                              as.numeric((affect_rating_segment - 4) < 0)),

      lab_id = lab_id,
      subj_id = subj_id,
      folder = folder,
      file_name = file_name,
      segment_start_sec = segment_start_sec,
      segment_end_sec = segment_end_sec,
      duration_sec = duration_sec,
      duration_msec = duration_msec,
      happiness_full_video = happiness_full_video,
      surprise_full_video = surprise_full_video,
      trial_num = trial_num,
      age_days = age_days,
      age_group = age_group,
      condition = condition,
      cb_order = cb_order,
      helper_hinderer_choice = helper_hinderer_choice,
      push_up_identity = push_up_identity,
      push_up_order = push_up_order,
      push_up_side = push_up_side,
      meet_eligibility = meet_eligibility
    )
  },
  by = .I
]

time_series_data[, trial_num_int := as.integer(trial_num)]
time_series_data[
  ,
  is_push_up :=
    (push_up_order == "first"  & trial_num_int %% 2L == 1L) |
    (push_up_order == "second" & trial_num_int %% 2L == 0L)
]
time_series_data[
  ,
  helping := factor(
    ifelse(is.na(is_push_up), NA_character_,
           ifelse(is_push_up, "help", "hinder")),
    levels = c("hinder", "help")
  )
]

# Summarize into 1s bins to get time course measures:
# - Avg rating (centered; neutral=0)
# - Proportion not neutral (non-zero)
# - Proportion positive / negative
timecourse_1s <- time_series_data[
  ,
  .(
    avg_rating_c0   = mean(affect_c0, na.rm = TRUE),
    prop_nonneutral = mean(nonneutral_01, na.rm = TRUE),
    prop_pos        = mean(pos_01, na.rm = TRUE),
    prop_neg        = mean(neg_01, na.rm = TRUE),
    n_obs           = sum(!is.na(affect_c0))
  ),
  by = .(condition, helping, time_sec)
]

# Plot average affect rating over time (centered; 0 = neutral)
p_time_affect_avg <- ggplot(
  timecourse_1s,
  aes(x = time_sec, y = avg_rating_c0, color = helping)
) +
  #geom_jitter(alpha = 0.3, size = 1) +
  geom_smooth(
    method = "loess",
    se = TRUE,
    aes(fill = helping),
    alpha = 0.2
  ) +
  facet_grid(rows = vars(condition)) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    x = "Time (sec)",
    y = "Average affect rating (centered; 0 = neutral)",
    color = "Helping condition",
    fill  = "Helping condition"
  )

# Plot proportion non-neutral over time
p_time_affect_prop_nonneutral <- ggplot(
  timecourse_1s,
  aes(x = time_sec, y = prop_nonneutral, color = helping)
) +
  #geom_jitter(alpha = 0.3, size = 1) +
  geom_smooth(
    method = "loess",
    se = TRUE,
    aes(fill = helping),
    alpha = 0.2
  ) +
  facet_grid(rows = vars(condition)) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    x = "Time (sec)",
    y = "Proportion non-neutral",
    color = "Helping condition",
    fill  = "Helping condition"
  )

# Plot proportion positive over time
p_time_affect_prop_pos <- ggplot(
  timecourse_1s,
  aes(x = time_sec, y = prop_pos, color = helping)
) +
  #geom_jitter(alpha = 0.3, size = 1) +
  geom_smooth(
    method = "loess",
    se = TRUE,
    aes(fill = helping),
    alpha = 0.2
  ) +
  facet_grid(rows = vars(condition)) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    x = "Time (sec)",
    y = "Proportion positive",
    color = "Helping condition",
    fill  = "Helping condition"
  )

# Plot proportion negative over time
p_time_affect_prop_neg <- ggplot(
  timecourse_1s,
  aes(x = time_sec, y = prop_neg, color = helping)
) +
  #geom_jitter(alpha = 0.3, size = 1) +
  geom_smooth(
    method = "loess",
    se = TRUE,
    aes(fill = helping),
    alpha = 0.2
  ) +
  facet_grid(rows = vars(condition)) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    x = "Time (sec)",
    y = "Proportion negative",
    color = "Helping condition",
    fill  = "Helping condition"
  )

# Print plots
p_time_affect_avg
p_time_affect_prop_nonneutral
p_time_affect_prop_pos
p_time_affect_prop_neg


```
